Solana token scanner with chart and alert handoff

A useful token scanner should make a contract address easier to understand: chart, liquidity, market cap, metadata, risk notes, Telegram alerts, and terminal actions in one path.

Scan in terminalRug checker

What this workflow covers

CA lookup

Paste a Solana contract address and move into token context quickly.

Market data

Review market cap, liquidity, pair status, and chart handoff.

Risk context

Surface visible warnings without pretending any scan can prove safety.

Telegram flow

Turn scans into group cards, alerts, and follow-up proof links.

Trade handoff

Move from scanner to chart, preset, position, and PnL review.

Fresh launches

Use scanners to inspect new pairs before the chat feed outruns them.

FAQ

What should a Solana token scanner show?

A useful Solana token scanner should show token identity, market data, liquidity, chart links, visible risks, social or metadata context, and next actions.

Is a token scanner the same as a rug checker?

They overlap. A scanner gathers token context, while a rug-check style view focuses on risks such as liquidity, authorities, holder concentration, and metadata warnings.

Can a token scanner prove a coin is safe?

No. Token scanners can surface useful warnings and context, but memecoin trading remains risky and users must make their own decisions.
